document.title: Software Validation Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for XAS_W software and firmware used with A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software verification, validation, traceability, hazard analysis, cybersecurity controls, and unresolved anomaly documentation for the detector software technical file.
keyPoints: The document concludes that a Moderate level of concern applies to XAS_W software.
XAS_W performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing for the detector.
The report includes module verification, system validation, traceability analysis, hazard analysis, cybersecurity requirements, and unresolved anomaly status.
System testing states that all module functions are adequate and no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency were found.

concepts.name: Software validation report
concepts.evidence: Software Validation Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Moderate level of concern
concepts.evidence: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the XAS_W software.

concepts.name: Cyber Security
concepts.evidence: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

concepts.name: Hazard Analysis
concepts.evidence: The following chart shows a list of potential hazards that may occur during use of XAS_W.

concepts.name: Unresolved Anomalies

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-131 with revision 0.
The covered detector models are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between A1717MCW and A1417MCW and F1417MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: The models have the same performance and usage except for size, with no difference in software validation.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Software Validation' (A-TCF -5-131)

evidence.claim: XAS_W performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: XAS_W is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The software level of concern is Moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the XAS_W software. Level of Concern : Moderate

evidence.claim: The document references FDA software premarket submission guidance dated May 11, 2005.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The development process is stated to fulfill 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: XAS_W follows a classic waterfall software development methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at XAS_W follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include limiting wireless network access by ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include protecting transmitted data using encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Module verification was evaluated as successful on a standard PC.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found all module functions adequate and no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies section lists no unresolved anomalies.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: Software version 2.3.0.3 for XAS_W is listed as an initial release dated Nov/15/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Date Name Description 2.3.0.3 Nov/15/2022 XAS_W Initial release.

evidence.claim: Firmware versions are listed for A1417MCW, A1717MCW, and F1417MCW as initial releases dated Nov/15/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Firmware version info ( A1417MCW ) 1.1.0.2.23 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release ... Firmware version info ( A1717MCW ) 1.1.0.2.23 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release ... Firmware version info ( F1417MCW ) 2.0.0.1 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release

reviewFlags.reason: Document date evidence is inconsistent: early headers show Rev. Date Nov/23/2022, test pages show Rev. Date Nov/10/2022, and later pages show Rev. Date Nov/15/2022.
Authority signal in metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, but the provided source text explicitly references FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 and does not explicitly mention MFDS.
The source text contains OCR or transcription issues such as misspellings and inconsistent numbering, including FRIMWARE, Pwr buttin, and HA-005.
